sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, a triathlete training for a triathlon, presents with chronic heel pain, specifically in the back of the heel, radiating up into the calf, which worsens after running, biking, or swimming, and is also present in the morning upon waking.
The pain is described as tight and tearing, and eases with movement but returns with prolonged sitting or activity.
The patient denies pain in the left leg, but reports pain on the right side, particularly with toe dorsiflexion and heel compression.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's lower extremities were examined, with the doctor noting that the Thompson test was negative.
The doctor also found tenderness on the right side, specifically along the Achilles tendon, particularly with compression and dorsiflexion of the foot.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has achilles tendinitis, likely due to overuse from aggressive triathlon training.
Problem list: Achilles tendinitis; status: confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 3 tablets every 6 hours for inflammation.
I ordered 2 physical therapy visits to improve strength and stretching knowledge, particularly in the lower extremities.
I instructed the patient to stop training for 1-2 weeks to allow for healing.
I asked the patient to return in 7-10 days after the first physical therapy appointment to monitor progress.
